nodes.name: P01
nodes.title: Salt-tolerant genes from halophytes are potential key players of salt tolerance in crops
nodes.summary: A review surveying halophyte-derived salt-tolerance genes (SOS1, NHX, HKT family members, H+-ATPases) and their track record when overexpressed in crop species such as rice, soybean, tomato and alfalfa.

nodes.name: P02
nodes.title: The ThSOS3 Gene Improves the Salt Tolerance of Transgenic Tamarix hispida and Arabidopsis thaliana
nodes.summary: Cloned five ThSOS genes from Tamarix hispida, a woody halophyte able to form natural stands in ~1% salt soils, and showed ThSOS3 overexpression raises salt tolerance in transgenic Tamarix and Arabidopsis.

nodes.name: P03
nodes.title: The SbSOS1 gene from the extreme halophyte Salicornia brachiata enhances Na+ loading in xylem and confers salt tolerance in transgenic tobacco
nodes.summary: Characterized the SOS pathway (SOS3-SOS2-SOS1) in the extreme halophyte Salicornia brachiata and demonstrated that SbSOS1 overexpression in tobacco improves xylem Na+ loading and salt tolerance.

nodes.name: P04
nodes.title: SOS3 from Avicennia marina Enhances Salt Stress Tolerance of Arabidopsis thaliana
nodes.summary: Structural and functional analysis of AmSOS3 from the mangrove Avicennia marina showed it complements an Arabidopsis sos3 mutant and, when overexpressed, boosts salinity tolerance.

nodes.name: P05
nodes.title: Constitutive high-level SOS1 expression and absence of HKT1;1 expression in the salt-accumulating halophyte Salicornia dolichostachya
nodes.summary: Compared gene expression in the highly salt-accumulating Salicornia dolichostachya against its glycophytic relative Spinacia oleracea, finding constitutively elevated SOS1 and undetectable HKT1;1, pointing to enhanced xylem Na+ loading combined with suppressed xylem Na+ retrieval as the accumulation strategy.

nodes.name: P06
nodes.title: Characterization of Salt Overly Sensitive 1 (SOS1) gene homoeologs in quinoa
nodes.summary: First molecular characterization of SOS1 homoeologs in the allotetraploid halophytic food crop quinoa, noting quinoa likely uses distinct mechanisms from other well-studied halophytes to reach its salt tolerance.

nodes.name: P07
nodes.title: Overexpression of NtSOS2 From Halophyte Plant N. tangutorum Enhances Tolerance to Salt Stress in Arabidopsis
nodes.summary: Cloned NtSOS2 from the desert shrub Nitraria tangutorum (collected in Inner Mongolia) and showed its expression in Arabidopsis improves germination, growth and salt tolerance relative to wild type.

nodes.name: P08
nodes.title: Coevolutionary Patterns in SOS1 and NHX1: Insights into Plant Ion Homeostasis Proteins
nodes.summary: A comparative/coevolutionary analysis of SOS1 and NHX1 across plant lineages, framing Na+ exclusion and vacuolar sequestration as the two core ion-homeostasis strategies distinguishing halophytes from glycophytes.

nodes.name: P09
nodes.title: SsHKT1;1 is coordinated with SsSOS1 and SsNHX1 to regulate Na+ homeostasis in Suaeda salsa under saline conditions
nodes.summary: Showed SsHKT1;1 is a plasma-membrane Na+-selective transporter expressed in root stele tissue, and that its expression is coordinated with SsSOS1 and SsNHX1 to jointly manage the large Na+ loads Suaeda salsa accumulates in saline soil.

nodes.name: P10
nodes.title: Molecular characterization and functional analysis of a vacuolar Na/H antiporter gene (HcNHX1) from Halostachys caspica
nodes.summary: Cloned and characterized a tonoplast Na+/H+ antiporter from the halophyte Halostachys caspica, part of a broader set of NHX1 orthologs reported across Suaeda, Thellungiella, Salicornia and Nitraria species.

nodes.name: P11
nodes.title: Cloning and characterization of SucNHX1, a novel vacuolar Na+/H+ antiporter from the halophyte Suaeda corniculata that enhances saline-alkali tolerance in Arabidopsis by overexpression
nodes.summary: Cloned SucNHX1 from Suaeda corniculata (a species also used as a wild vegetable, with oil-rich seeds) and confirmed overexpression in Arabidopsis gives combined saline-alkaline tolerance and a higher leaf K+/Na+ ratio.

nodes.name: P12
nodes.title: Salt-Enhanced Reproductive Development of Suaeda salsa L. Coincided With Ion Transporter Gene Upregulation in Flowers and Increased Pollen K+ Content
nodes.summary: Found that 200 mM NaCl treatment actually increased flower and seed number in Suaeda salsa, coinciding with upregulated ion-transporter gene expression in flowers and a rise in pollen K+ content -- salinity aiding rather than harming reproduction in this species.

nodes.name: P13
nodes.title: The Vacuolar Na+/H+ Antiporter Gene SsNHX1 from the Halophyte Salsola soda Confers Salt Tolerance in Transgenic Alfalfa
nodes.summary: Isolated SsNHX1 from Salsola soda and showed that expressing it in the forage crop alfalfa let transgenic plants grow in up to 400 mM NaCl for 50 days, well beyond untransformed alfalfa's tolerance.

nodes.name: P14
nodes.title: Nitrogen-Salt Interaction Adjusts Root Development and Ion Accumulation of the Halophyte Suaeda salsa
nodes.summary: A soil-column study of Suaeda salsa under combined nitrogen and salt treatments, finding moderate nitrogen application improves root growth and Na+/Cl- uptake at low-to-moderate salinity, relevant to using the species in saline-soil amelioration programs.

nodes.name: P15
nodes.title: Salt balance of leaves of the mangrove Avicennia marina
nodes.summary: Classic tracer study finding root ultrafiltration excludes roughly 80% of the salt moving toward Avicennia marina's roots, with leaf salt glands excreting up to 40% of what still reaches the leaves -- establishing root filtration as the dominant mechanism, glands as a secondary one.

nodes.name: P16
nodes.title: Ultrastructure of the salt glands of the mangrove Avicennia marina
nodes.summary: Ultrastructural study describing the multicellular salt gland (collecting, stalk and excretory cells) of Avicennia marina and proposing an eccrine-type secretion route through the endoplasmic reticulum of the stalk and excretory cells.

nodes.name: P17
nodes.title: A reference-grade genome identifies salt-tolerance genes from the salt-secreting mangrove species Avicennia marina
nodes.summary: A reference-grade genome of Avicennia marina used to identify candidate salt-tolerance genes underlying its combined root-filtration, gland-secretion, osmotic and antioxidant defenses against seawater salinity.

nodes.name: P18
nodes.title: Identification of salt gland-associated genes and characterization of a dehydrin from the salt secretor mangrove Avicennia officinalis
nodes.summary: Used subtractive hybridization on salt-gland-rich tissue of Avicennia officinalis to identify genes preferentially expressed there, spanning metabolic, stress-response, transcription and transport functional classes, including a dehydrin gene.

nodes.name: P19
nodes.title: Dynamic secretion changes in the salt glands of Avicennia officinalis in response to a changing saline environment
nodes.summary: Used an epidermal-peel assay to track hundreds of individual salt glands on Avicennia officinalis and found gland secretion responds dynamically (including rhythmic fluctuation blocked by an aquaporin inhibitor) to rapidly changing ambient salinity.

nodes.name: P20
nodes.title: Salt extrusion, a new function of lenticels in red mangrove leaves
nodes.summary: Reports that Rhizophora mangle, a mangrove lacking dedicated salt glands, instead uses leaf lenticels as a previously unrecognized route for salt extrusion -- an alternative to the gland-based strategy seen in Avicennia species.

nodes.name: P21
nodes.title: Effect of salinity on osmotic adjustment, glycinebetaine accumulation and betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase gene expression in Salicornia europaea and Suaeda maritima
nodes.summary: Compared glycine betaine accumulation and betaine aldehyde dehydrogenase gene expression under increasing salinity in Salicornia europaea and Suaeda maritima, though did not find a statistically significant proline increase with salt level in this particular study.

nodes.name: P22
nodes.title: The growth and accumulation of osmotic solutes of halophytic weeds
nodes.summary: Notes that common glasswort Salicornia europaea, notable for actually growing poorly at low NaCl, can survive salinities up to 1,020 mM with no toxic effect, growing best in the 136-510 mM range, and accumulates proline and glycine betaine as part of its osmotic adjustment.

nodes.name: P23
nodes.title: Salicornia bigelovii Torr.: An Oilseed Halophyte for Seawater Irrigation
nodes.summary: Six-year field trial irrigating Salicornia bigelovii directly with seawater in a coastal desert, finding seed and biomass yields matching or exceeding freshwater oilseed crops like soybean and sunflower, with seed oil suitable for replacing soybean oil in poultry feed.

nodes.name: P24
nodes.title: ICBA achieves progress in breaking Salicornia yield ceiling
nodes.summary: ICBA breeding trials across 16 Salicornia genotypes under seawater irrigation in the UAE report seed oil content up to 38% and yields reaching 3 tonnes/hectare, aimed at food, forage and biofuel uses.

nodes.name: P25
nodes.title: New study finds brackish water best for Salicornia cultivation
nodes.summary: Field comparison of fresh, brackish, seawater and hypersaline irrigation on Salicornia europaea found brackish water (25 dS/m) gave the best biomass and seed yield, while full seawater roughly halved germination and hypersaline water nearly eliminated it -- useful for smallholder cultivation planning.

nodes.name: P26
nodes.title: Halotolerant Marine Rhizosphere-Competent Actinobacteria Promote Salicornia bigelovii Growth and Seed Production Using Seawater Irrigation
nodes.summary: Tested halotolerant rhizosphere Actinobacteria as growth-promoting inoculants for seawater-irrigated Salicornia bigelovii in the UAE, aimed at improving yield for its established food, feed and biofuel uses.

nodes.name: P27
nodes.title: A high-quality chromosome-level Eutrema salsugineum genome, an extremophile plant model
nodes.summary: Long-read chromosome-level genome assembly of Eutrema salsugineum (formerly Thellungiella salsuginea), a Brassicaceae halophyte able to withstand 500 mM NaCl and -15C cold while still reproducing, used widely as an Arabidopsis-related extremophile model.

nodes.name: P28
nodes.title: Insights into salt tolerance from the genome of Thellungiella salsuginea
nodes.summary: Draft genome (7 chromosomes, ~28,457 genes) of the Arabidopsis relative Thellungiella salsuginea, highlighting expanded gene families related to cation transport, ABA signaling and wax production as likely contributors to its stress tolerance.

nodes.name: P29
nodes.title: The Reference Genome of the Halophytic Plant Eutrema salsugineum
nodes.summary: Notes that E. salsugineum's salt tolerance appears to come from enhancing mechanisms already present in glycophytes rather than novel morphology -- for instance, reducing expression of its SOS1 ortholog sharply cuts its salt tolerance, suggesting related crop species could be engineered the same way.

nodes.name: P30
nodes.title: Anastatica hierochuntica, an Arabidopsis Desert Relative, Is Tolerant to Multiple Abiotic Stresses and Shares Stress Strategies with Eutrema salsugineum
nodes.summary: Compared the desert Brassicaceae species Anastatica hierochuntica with Eutrema salsugineum, finding shared strategies (tight shoot Na+ control, constitutive antioxidant upregulation) alongside species-specific ones, positioning A. hierochuntica as a second useful desert-adapted Arabidopsis relative.

nodes.name: P31
nodes.title: Genomic Variation Landscape of the Model Salt Cress Eutrema salsugineum
nodes.summary: Resequenced 90 wild Eutrema salsugineum genomes across its Asian and North American range and detected selection signals in genes tied to salt and cold tolerance, distinguishing species-wide adaptation from population-local adaptation.

props.note: reproductive-stage NaCl treatment used in the experiment, not an upper limit
transgenic alfalfa expressing SsNHX1 tolerated up to 400 mM NaCl over 50 days
described as growing well up to 400 mM NaCl; used as a model for saline-alkali soil improvement
reported to survive up to 1,020 mM NaCl with no phytotoxic effect; best growth 136-510 mM
survives exposure to 500 mM NaCl (and cold to -15C) while still reproducing normally
